untranslatable_reason: Ascending Double Bottom (AADB) is a visual candlestick pattern that requires shape recognition (two equal lows with higher middle point). This cannot be expressed using only price, volume, and technical indicator operators. The pattern structure itself—identifying the two bottoms, measuring their proximity, and confirming the ascending structure—requires pattern detection logic not available in the allowed indicators. Donchian channels used as proxy for resistance/support breakout detection, but this does not capture the AADB pattern formation requirement.
translation_notes: Signal references pattern-based entry (ascending double bottom structure with upward breakout) and provides statistical performance data (95% rise 5%+ in bull markets, 93% in bear markets, 40% average rise). Entry modeled as breakout above recent resistance (dc_upper). Exit condition not specified by author, so defensive reversal below support (dc_lower) applied. Stop loss set conservatively at 5% based on stated 5% failure rate. True pattern recognition would require additional pattern-detection indicators not in the allowed set.
